message:
docs: update issue tracking for SendGrid provisioning and E2E testing

- Close SendGrid provisioning automation issue (dlzr.2) with
  completion timestamp
- Move E2E compliance run (dlzr.4) to in_progress reflecting
  current test execution state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.3","title":"[FWC] History UX: compare and diff surfaces across result handles and prior runs","description":"# [FWC] History UX: compare and diff surfaces across result handles and prior runs

## Goal
Make it easy to understand what changed between two operations or two outputs without exporting them into an external diff workflow every time.

## Scope
- compare two runs, two result handles, or two projections of the same handle
- semantic summaries and field-level diffs where available
- redaction-aware diff behavior for sensitive values
- compact and machine-readable diff representations suitable for docs, tests, and approvals

## Acceptance criteria
- agents can reason about behavioral changes, drift, or replay consequences directly inside `fwc`
- compare output remains useful even when sensitive fields are redacted","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.284317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:49:58.925884Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","compare","diff","fwc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.284317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.284317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.284317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.284317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:49:58.925839Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","title":"[FWC] History UX: reversal and undo guidance with connector-aware safety boundaries","description":"# [FWC] History UX: reversal and undo guidance with connector-aware safety boundaries

## Goal
Tell the truth about reversal. Where a connector advertises a real reversible action, `fwc` should help the caller find and preflight it. Where no safe reversal exists, `fwc` should say so clearly instead of implying an undo fantasy.

## Scope
- capability-aware reversal guidance and explicit non-reversible classifications
- links from history or receipts to the appropriate reversal or repair path when it exists
- approval and safety boundaries for reversal actions that themselves are risky
- error and explanation surfaces for unsupported or unsafe undo attempts

## Acceptance criteria
- callers can quickly tell whether a safe way back exists after an action
- reversal guidance reduces operational fear without making false promises about undoability","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.481341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:04:42.563506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","fwc","history","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.481341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.22.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.481341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.481341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:07.481341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:49:58.993583Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:04:42.563467Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","title":"[FWC] History UX: pending approvals, blocked actions, and resumable work queue","description":"# [FWC] History UX: pending approvals, blocked actions, and resumable work queue

## Goal
Give agents and operators one explicit place to inspect work that is waiting on approval, blocked by an earlier preflight, or no longer runnable because its approval context expired or changed.

## Scope
- list and filter pending, approved, denied, expired, superseded, and resumed approval-backed actions by connector, operation, zone, profile, risk, and age
- show the origin preflight, macro, or pipeline stage, linked receipts or handles, and the exact resume or abandon command
- compact blocked-work summaries with drill-down paths rather than forcing users to reconstruct state from scattered receipts
- clear queue semantics for both primitive actions and higher-level workflow actions

## Acceptance criteria
- a caller can answer \"what is waiting, what expired, and how do I continue or discard it?\" from `fwc` alone
- approval-backed work no longer disappears into hidden preflight state or half-remembered receipts","notes":"Treat this as the explicit answer to approval-state drift. It should unify primitive and workflow actions, expose expiry or supersession clearly, and make resume or abandon commands obvious. Cover it with fast snapshots, host-backed integration, confusion-corpus cases, operator and agent E2E playbooks, and debugging docs with detailed structured logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:51.222623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.620836Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["approvals","cli","fwc","history"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.187154Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.22.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.401478Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:51.222623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:52.620801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","title":"[FWC] Host-first truthful runtime migration: no silent fallbacks, no fake auth, no simulated control paths","description":"# [FWC] Host-first truthful runtime migration: no silent fallbacks, no fake auth, no simulated control paths

## Goal
Make `fwc` the canonical agent-native shell over live `fcp-host` truth for runtime control, while keeping offline file/package/policy workflows explicit and honest.

## Why this branch exists
The current `fwc` implementation already has strong agent ergonomics, but it still mixes live host-backed behavior with workspace-manifest fallbacks, placeholder auth paths, assumed simulation support, and a few planning-only runtime verbs. That combination is good enough for demos and partial workflows, but it is not yet the "real control plane for all of FCP" standard we want.

## Problem statement
Today an agent can sometimes believe it is operating the live system when it is actually reading static manifests, mutating a local file that still requires restart, using placeholder capability tokens, or seeing metadata that was guessed by the host instead of truthfully reported by the connector/runtime.

This branch exists to remove that ambiguity.

## Scope
- define the hard runtime boundary between live host truth and explicit offline artifact work
- complete the host admin surface for lifecycle/config/install/reload/logs/events/mesh/registry control
- replace fake auth/approval paths with real capability issuance, holder proof, and verification
- add a real host-routed simulate path and stop advertising support by assumption
- make search/template/validate/export-tools/serve-mcp host-authoritative for live runtime usage
- remove synthetic metadata defaults so unknown remains unknown
- make workflow/intent layers refuse or clearly label any runtime verb that is not actually backed by the host
- prove the result through host-backed integration, E2E evidence, and docs/playbooks","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:24:48.166767Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:24:48.166767Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","host-first","planning","runtime","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:24:48.166767Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","title":"[FWC] Connector/host readiness contract for discovery, config, lifecycle, and invocation","description":"# [FWC] Connector/host readiness contract for discovery, config, lifecycle, and invocation

## Goal
Define the minimum metadata and RPC surface that a connector and `fcp-host` must expose in order for `fwc` to support it cleanly.

## Why this exists
The current workspace has real connector crates, but metadata quality is uneven: some areas are richly introspectable while others still rely on simulated CLI output or connector-local conventions. `fwc` needs a strict readiness contract so we know what must be standardized instead of hand-waving around gaps.

## The contract should cover
### Host-visible connector summary
- canonical connector id, display name, version, categories/archetypes
- enabled/disabled state and health state
- installation/pinning status
- capability/risk summary and operation counts

### Operation metadata
- operation id and human summary
- input schema and output schema
- capability requirement, risk level, safety tier, idempotency class
- approval requirements and simulate support
- examples/agent hints for the happy path and common failure modes

### Config metadata
- config schema with secrets clearly marked/redacted
- default values, required fields, and secretless alternatives (`credential_id` paths)
- doctor/self-check expectations after configure
- import/export representation rules

### Lifecycle/state metadata
- whether the connector supports streaming/events
- whether it is configured, handshaken, running, degraded, or blocked
- what host actions are legal from the current state

## Deliverables
- A formal readiness checklist for connectors and host endpoints.
- Canonical payload shapes for discovery/show/config/invoke/lifecycle flows.
- A list of mandatory fields that `fwc` can rely on without connector-specific hacks.
- Explicit gap categories so later cohort beads can remediate missing coverage systematically.

## Acceptance criteria
- We can look at any connector and determine objectively whether it is `fwc`-ready.
- The contract is strong enough to drive generated help/examples and dynamic command aliasing.
- The contract avoids manual per-connector shims except where a justified compatibility layer is intentionally specified.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.627638Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","updated_at":"2026-03-08T01:31:28.535895Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T01:31:14.903224Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fwc","host","schemas"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.627638Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:24.629879Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:25.276224Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1039,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the connector/host readiness contract as crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs. Deliverables: (1) Formal readiness checklist — ReadinessVerdict with SummaryReadiness, OperationsReadiness, ConfigReadiness, LifecycleReadiness areas, (2) Canonical payload shapes — ConnectorSummary, ConnectorDetail, OperationSummary, HealthSummary, RateLimitSummary for discovery/show/config/invoke/lifecycle, (3) Mandatory field constants — MANDATORY_SUMMARY_FIELDS, MANDATORY_OPERATION_FIELDS, RECOMMENDED_OPERATION_FIELDS, (4) Gap categories — GapCategory enum (Identity, OperationMetadata, ConfigSchema, Lifecycle, AgentHints, EventSupport, RateLimits, ApprovalPolicy) with GapSeverity (Blocking, Degraded, Cosmetic), (5) ConnectorCohort enum for grouping remediation work, (6) evaluate_introspection() function that produces ReadinessVerdict from raw introspection JSON, (7) 23 unit tests covering serialization round-trips, evaluation logic, edge cases, severity ordering. Total fwc tests: 245, clippy clean. — SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:21:09Z"},{"id":1042,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Verified complete: readiness.rs (2,720 lines, 88 tests) implements full readiness contract. ReadinessVerdict (Ready/PartiallyReady/NotReady) with per-area checklists (Summary, Operations, Config, Lifecycle). Mandatory fields checklist for all connectors. Gap categories for systematic cohort remediation. Covers canonical_id, display_name, archetypes, semver, description, operation_count, risk_summary, schema availability, secret redaction, enable/disable support. All 454 fwc tests pass, clippy clean. — SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:31:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","title":"[FWC] Host admin API for connector inventory, lifecycle, config, invoke, and audit-backed state changes","description":"# [FWC] Host admin API for connector inventory, lifecycle, config, invoke, and audit-backed state changes

## Goal
Extend `fcp-host` with the concrete management/API surface that `fwc` needs so the standalone CLI can operate on real installed connectors rather than simulated data.

## Background
`fcp-host` already owns orchestration and discovery is under active work, but `fwc` needs a broader admin plane: lifecycle transitions, config reads/writes, invoke/simulate bridges, status inspection, and audit-friendly state changes.

## Scope
- Inventory/search/show endpoints for connectors and operations.
- Lifecycle endpoints for install/verify/enable/disable/start/stop/restart/update/pin/unpin/status.
- Config endpoints for schema/get/set/unset/import/export/validate/doctor.
- Invoke/simulate/batch/stream/log-tail endpoints or equivalent RPC methods.
- Audit/event semantics for state-changing admin actions.

## Critical constraint
This bead must eliminate simulated/demo connector CLI data as a required dependency for `fwc`. The host is the runtime source of truth.

## Deliverables
- Endpoint/RPC definitions with stable request/response payloads.
- Permission/policy model for admin actions vs connector operations.
- Deterministic error codes and reason payloads for all admin actions.
- Structured logging and audit emission rules for config/lifecycle changes.
- Test harness hooks so the later `fwc` integration beads can exercise real host flows.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fwc` can rely on host APIs instead of duplicating connector-local logic.
- State changes are auditable and recoverable.
- The management surface is sufficiently complete that `fwc` does not need direct filesystem or process hacks to operate connectors.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.758334Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:28.510280Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fwc","host","management"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.758334Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:26.680728Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:27.029763Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2h7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:28.100678Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:27.555521Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-y1q8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:28.510186Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","title":"[FWC] Host admin API: inventory and operation introspection RPC contract","description":"# [FWC] Host admin API: inventory and operation introspection RPC contract

## Goal
Define the host-backed inventory and operation-descriptor payloads that make every connector and every operation discoverable from `fcp-host` without `fwc` maintaining bespoke command tables.

## Scope
- connector inventory fields for provider, cohort, archetype, risk, capabilities, health, install state, and availability
- operation descriptors for input schema, output schema, examples, approvals, streaming/log support, and idempotency hints
- filter/search/pagination/cache semantics so discovery stays token-efficient at workspace scale
- explicit representation of metadata gaps so the CLI can surface missing readiness cleanly instead of guessing

## Deliverables
- stable request/response contract for inventory, show, ops, schema, and example reads
- canonical field glossary shared by CLI rendering, completions, workflow planning, and docs

## Acceptance criteria
- `fwc list/show/ops/schema/examples` can be driven from host inventory alone
- missing or partial connector metadata becomes explicit and testable rather than silently papered over","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzePrairie","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T00:01:55.575110Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T00:01:55.575096Z","close_reason":"Completed: fwc host-backed list/show/ops/schema/examples wired to fcp-host inventory/introspection RPCs with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fwc","host","introspection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2h7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:13.721998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1505,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4.1","author":"BronzePrairie","text":"Implemented host-backed fwc list/show/ops/schema/examples --host <endpoint> using fcp-host /rpc/discover, /rpc/connectors/{id}, and /rpc/introspect/{id} while preserving manifest-backed defaults when --host is absent. Added host-path selector resolution, host metadata gap reporting, and focused mock-host tests for list/show/ops/schema/examples. Verification so far: targeted rch cargo check for fwc passed; targeted host-path fwc tests passed; cargo clippy -p fwc --all-targets -- -D warnings is currently blocked by pre-existing errors in crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs; workspace cargo check --workspace --all-targets is blocked by pre-existing fcp-registry errors converting fcp_cbor::SerializationError into RegistryError.","created_at":"2026-03-10T23:59:07Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.1","title":"fcp.sendgrid: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.

Hash: a70876e5fbdeb908a5567993f3085faf382c2282c9bd167ff54a3d026af3a650. Good+bad test vectors created.","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:15:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.2","title":"fcp.sendgrid: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:13:55.038974560Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-09T07:19:50.174001Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T07:19:50.173987Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1160,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Provisioning automation: recipe, base_url_policy, SelfCheckReport, 17 new tests (API key). Commit 93f29ae1.","created_at":"2026-03-09T07:19:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dlzr.3","title":"TEST: fcp.sendgrid Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dpo","title":"fcp.twilio: Voice calls with outbound and IVR support","description":"# fcp.twilio: Voice calls with outbound and IVR support (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe voice automation via Twilio:
- initiate outbound calls
- manage call status and termination
- generate constrained TwiML for IVR-style flows

Voice calls are high-impact side effects; treat as approval-gated by policy.

## Scope
### Outbound call control
- Create outbound call to a destination.
- Query call status.
- Hang up a call.
- Optional: conference creation/management (defer if too large).

### TwiML (constrained)
- Generate TwiML responses from a safe template set:
  - `<Say>`, `<Gather>`, `<Dial>`, `<Redirect>`
- Explicitly avoid arbitrary injection of external URLs/scripts.

### Recording/transcription (optional)
- Accessing recordings is sensitive; treat as dangerous with explicit capability.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.voice.call` (dangerous)
- `twilio.voice.read`
- `twilio.voice.hangup` (dangerous)
- `twilio.voice.twiml.generate`
- Optional: `twilio.voice.recordings.read` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Twilio API host(s).
- For call initiation/termination:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates
  - emit receipts + audit events with call SID + destination hash (never raw phone number)
- Strict log redaction for phone numbers, call recordings, and transcribed content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.voice.call`
  - `twilio.voice.get`
  - `twilio.voice.hangup`
  - `twilio.voice.twiml.render`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - call create/get/hangup request formation
  - TwiML template rendering
  - approval gating paths
  - log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Voice operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and audited.
- TwiML generation is constrained and cannot be used as an injection vector.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ds4","title":"fcp.zendesk: SLA Tracking","description":"# fcp.zendesk: SLA Tracking (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose SLA state and warnings so agents can:
- monitor SLA compliance
- prioritize tickets at risk of breach
- trigger escalation workflows safely

## Scope
### Read
- Retrieve SLA policy metadata (where permitted).
- For a ticket:
  - current SLA status
  - time-to-breach estimates

### Event ingestion (optional)
- Ingest SLA breach warning events via webhook if Zendesk supports it.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.sla.read`
- `zendesk.webhook.ingest` (if SLA events are delivered)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- SLA events are tainted external inputs.
- No PII in logs.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.sla.ticket_status`
  - Optional: `zendesk.webhook.sla_event_ingest`
- Reason codes for escalation suggestions (not auto-actions).

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - SLA status parsing
  - webhook replay/idempotency (if events exist)

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably detect SLA risk without leaking ticket content.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dz01","title":"FCP V2 Connector Compliance Checklist (NORMATIVE)","description":"# FCP V2 Connector Compliance Checklist (NORMATIVE)

## Goal
Provide a single authoritative, V2-only checklist of all mechanical connector compliance requirements so new connectors can be reviewed for conformance without re-reading the spec/docs.

## Purpose
This checklist captures all NORMATIVE requirements from FCP Specification V2 and docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md that every connector MUST satisfy. Use this as a reference when creating or reviewing connector beads.

---

## 1. Connector Definition (NORMATIVE)

### Required Fields
Every connector MUST define:
- `id: ConnectorId` — Unique identifier (canonical: lowercase ASCII, `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9._:-]*$`)
- `name: String` — Human-readable name
- `version: Version` — Semantic version
- `format: ConnectorFormat` — Native or WASI
- `archetypes: Vec<ConnectorArchetype>` — From closed set
- `operations: Vec<Operation>` — All operations provided
- `events: Vec<EventType>` — Events emitted (if any)
- `required_caps: Vec<CapabilityId>` — Capabilities connector requires
- `optional_caps: Vec<CapabilityId>` — Optional capabilities
- `forbidden_caps: Vec<CapabilityId>` — Capabilities connector must NOT have

### ConnectorArchetype (Closed Set)
```rust
pub enum ConnectorArchetype {
    Bidirectional, // Sends and receives messages
    Streaming,     // Emits events (read-only)
    Operational,   // Executes operations (write)
    Storage,       // Stores/retrieves data
    Knowledge,     // Provides knowledge/search
}
```

### ConnectorFormat (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub enum ConnectorFormat {
    Native, // Requires OS-level sandboxing (seccomp, landlock, AppArmor)
    Wasi,   // RECOMMENDED for high-risk connectors (financial, credentials, external API)
}
```

**Guidance:**
- WASI format SHOULD be used for SafetyTier::Dangerous operations
- WASI provides memory isolation, capability-gated hostcalls, cross-platform consistency

---

## 2. Connector State Model (NORMATIVE)

### State Model Declaration
Every connector MUST declare one:
```rust
pub enum ConnectorStateModel {
    Stateless,           // No mesh-persisted state
    SingletonWriter,     // Exactly one writer via Lease
    Crdt { crdt_type },  // Multi-writer via CRDT (rare; justify)
}
```

### State Persistence Rules
- Connectors with polling/cursors/dedup caches MUST externalize state to mesh
- Local `$CONNECTOR_STATE` is cache only
- Authoritative state lives as mesh objects
- Single-writer connectors MUST use Lease for fencing

### Fork Detection (NORMATIVE for singleton_writer)
- Two `ConnectorStateObject` with same `prev` = SAFETY INCIDENT
- MUST pause connector execution
- MUST log fork event for audit

---

## 3. Zone Architecture (NORMATIVE)

### Single-Zone Binding
- Connector instance MUST bind to exactly ONE zone
- Cross-zone access requires explicit policy

### Standard Zone Hierarchy
```
z:owner         → Full owner access
z:private       → Personal data
z:work          → Work services
z:project:<name>→ Per-project isolation
z:community     → Semi-trusted communities
z:public        → Public/untrusted inputs
```

### Zone Rules
1. Default deny — If capability not granted to zone, impossible to invoke
2. No cross-connector calling — All composition through Gateway

---

## 4. Capability System (NORMATIVE)

### Capability Definition
```rust
pub struct Capability {
    pub id: CapabilityId,
    pub risk_level: RiskLevel,      // Low, Medium, High, Critical
    pub safety_tier: SafetyTier,    // Safe, Risky, Dangerous, Forbidden
    pub idempotency: IdempotencyClass,
    pub requires_approval: ApprovalMode,
    pub audit_level: AuditLevel,
    pub agent_hint: AgentHint,
}
```

### SafetyTier (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub enum SafetyTier {
    Safe,      // No approval needed
    Risky,     // Policy-based approval
    Dangerous, // Interactive human approval
    Forbidden, // Never allowed
}
```

### ApprovalMode (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub enum ApprovalMode {
    None,              // No approval needed
    Policy,            // Policy-based auto-approval
    Interactive,       // Human approval required
    ApprovalRequired,  // Requires ApprovalToken
}
```

---

## 5. NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)

### Default-Deny Allowlists
```rust
pub struct NetworkConstraints {
    pub host_allow: Vec<String>,     // Allowed hostnames
    pub port_allow: Vec<u16>,        // Allowed ports
    pub ip_allow: Vec<IpAddr>,       // Optional explicit IPs
    pub cidr_deny: Vec<String>,      // NORMATIVE defaults below
    pub deny_localhost: bool,        // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub deny_private_ranges: bool,   // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub deny_tailnet_ranges: bool,   // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub deny_ip_literals: bool,      // NORMATIVE default: true
    pub require_sni: bool,           // Enforce SNI match
    pub spki_pins: Vec<String>,      // Optional SPKI pins
    pub require_host_canonicalization: bool, // NORMATIVE default: true
}
```

### NORMATIVE CIDR Deny Defaults
- `127.0.0.0/8` (localhost)
- `::1` (IPv6 localhost)
- `169.254.0.0/16` (link-local)
- `10.0.0.0/8` (RFC1918)
- `172.16.0.0/12` (RFC1918)
- `192.168.0.0/16` (RFC1918)
- `100.64.0.0/10` (Tailnet ranges)

### Host Canonicalization (NORMATIVE)
- Hostnames MUST be lowercase
- IDNA2008 encoded
- No trailing dot
- Implementations MUST reject non-canonical forms

---

## 6. Egress Proxy & Credential Injection (NORMATIVE)

### Secretless Connectors (RECOMMENDED)
- Credentials injected by egress proxy at network boundary
- Raw secrets never enter connector memory
- Credentials declared via `credential_allow` in `CapabilityConstraints`

### CredentialObject (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub struct CredentialObject {
    pub credential_id: CredentialId,
    pub secret_id: SecretId,
    pub apply: CredentialApply,     // HttpHeader or QueryParam
    pub host_allow: Vec<String>,    // Defense-in-depth binding
}
```

### EgressHttpRequest (NORMATIVE)
```rust
pub struct EgressHttpRequest {
    pub method: String,
    pub url: String,
    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,
    pub body: Vec<u8>,
    pub credential: Option<CredentialId>, // Proxy injects this
}
```

---

## 7. Provenance and Taint (NORMATIVE)

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
bitflags! {
    pub struct TaintFlags: u32 {
        const NONE            = 0;
        const PUBLIC_INPUT    = 1 << 0;  // z:public, web
        const EXTERNAL_INPUT  = 1 << 1;  // Paired external identities
        const UNVERIFIED_LINK = 1 << 2;  // URLs/attachments not scanned
        const USER_SUPPLIED   = 1 << 3;  // Direct human input
        const PROMPT_SURFACE  = 1 << 4;  // Content interpreted by LLM
    }
}
```

### Taint Enforcement (NORMATIVE)
- Public-tainted input cannot directly drive Dangerous operations
- If `effective_taint() != NONE` and `operation.safety_tier >= Risky`, ApprovalToken required

### SanitizerReceipt (NORMATIVE)
- Machine-verifiable proof of sanitization
- Clears specific taints with evidence
- Connector version tracked for CVE auditing

---

## 8. Testing Requirements (NORMATIVE)

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- MUST NOT make real API calls
- Use wiremock or similar for HTTP mocking
- Test error conditions and rate limits
- Golden vectors for canonical serialization

### Integration Tests
- Mock external services
- Test multi-component scenarios
- Verify error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Run shared compliance runner
- Validate:
  - Default deny enforced
  - NetworkConstraints enforced
  - Receipts/audit for dangerous ops
  - Structured logs present
- Capture evidence bundle

---

## 9. Audit and Observability (NORMATIVE)

### AuditEvent Requirements
- All writes/dangerous ops MUST emit AuditEvent
- Correlation IDs for tracing
- No secrets in logs
- Structured JSON format

### Logging Requirements
- Never log tokens, passwords, PII
- Use reason codes for denials
- Include correlation IDs

---

## 10. Manifest Requirements

### Required Manifest Sections
```toml
[connector]
id = "fcp.<service>"
name = "Human Name"
version = "1.0.0"
archetypes = ["operational"]  # From closed set

[connector.state]
model = "singleton_writer"  # or "stateless" or "crdt"
state_schema_version = "1"

[connector.network]
host_allow = ["api.service.com"]
port_allow = [443]
deny_localhost = true
deny_private_ranges = true
```

### Sandbox Profile (NORMATIVE)
- Strict: No raw sockets, egress proxy only
- Moderate: Limited syscalls, proxy required
- Permissive: Full syscalls (avoid for sensitive connectors)

---

## Compliance Checklist Summary

### Connector Definition
- [ ] ConnectorId is canonical (lowercase, ASCII, regex-valid)
- [ ] Archetypes from closed set only
- [ ] Format specified (Native or WASI)
- [ ] WASI used for Dangerous operations

### State Management
- [ ] State model declared (Stateless/SingletonWriter/Crdt)
- [ ] State externalized to mesh (not local-only)
- [ ] Fork detection implemented for singleton_writer

### Security
- [ ] NetworkConstraints default-deny
- [ ] All NORMATIVE CIDR denies applied
- [ ] Host canonicalization enforced
- [ ] Credential injection via egress proxy (secretless)
- [ ] Taint tracking for external inputs

### Capabilities
- [ ] SafetyTier assigned per operation
- [ ] ApprovalMode defined
- [ ] AgentHints provided

### Testing
- [ ] Unit tests mock-only (no real API calls)
- [ ] E2E compliance runner passes
- [ ] Golden vectors for serialization

### Observability
- [ ] AuditEvents for dangerous ops
- [ ] Structured JSON logs
- [ ] No secrets logged

---

## References
- FCP_Specification_V2.md (canonical spec)
- docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md (connector guide)
- STANDARD: Connector Spec Template (flywheel_connectors-lszk.5)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checklist is V2-only and covers all NORMATIVE connector requirements from the spec + model connector guide.
- Checklist is kept in sync with `flywheel_connectors-1n78.1` and is used as the human-readable companion to the mechanical compliance runner.
- Each section is written so reviewers can mechanically verify compliance without relying on implicit assumptions.

Includes quick-reference checklist for reviewers and acceptance criteria. Unblocks 5 downstream beads including connector scaffold CLI and meta-connector EPICs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compliance","fcp2","normative","reference"]}
